items.mistake: Building a big string with `+=` inside a loop.
items.fix: Collect parts in a list, then call `''.join(parts)` once.
items.why: Strings are immutable; every `+=` allocates and copies — total work grows O(n²).
items.example:
```python
parts = []
for t in stream():
    parts.append(t)
text = ''.join(parts)
```

items.fix: Encode/decode at the boundary; pick one type and stick with it inside the program.
items.why: Concatenating, comparing or formatting across types raises `TypeError` or returns nonsense.
items.example:
```python
data = response.content              # bytes from HTTP
text = data.decode('utf-8')           # str for processing
```

items.mistake: Using `len(text)` to size a prompt for an LLM.
items.fix: Use the model's tokenizer (e.g. tiktoken) to count tokens.
items.why: One character can be many tokens (non-Latin scripts, emoji) or fractional; cost and context limits are per token.

items.mistake: Building user-facing SQL/HTML/JSON with f-strings or `+`.
items.fix: Use the right tool: parameterized queries, `html.escape`, `json.dumps`.
items.why: String concatenation around untrusted input causes injection vulnerabilities and malformed output.
items.example:
```python
import json
body = json.dumps({'q': user_text})   # safe, escaped
```

items.mistake: Calling `s.strip("prefix-")` to remove a literal prefix.
items.why: `strip` removes any character in the argument from both ends — not the literal substring.
